original: Ptolemy V, aged 13, is crowned King of Ptolemaic Egypt at Memphis by the High Priest of Ptah; the Rosetta Stone is inscribed afterward to proclaim this event
Greek astronomer and mathematician Ptolemy begins his observations of the heavens (until 141 AD)
Suintila, King of the Visigoths is overthrown after a reign of ten years, by Sisenand, a noble from Septimania
Cuthbert (later Saint Cuthbert) is consecrated Bishop of Lindisfarne by Archbishop Theodore at York
Stephen II is selected Catholic Pope after the death of Pope-elect Stephen
Jewish community in Cologne fasts to commemorate anti-Jewish violence
Lübeck accepts free Dutch ships into East Sea
Guru Amar Das becomes the Third Sikh Guru
University of Utrecht opening ceremony
The Quebec Seminary established by François de Montmorency-Laval, First Bishop of New France. North America's first institution of higher learning.
King Maximilian installed as land guardian of South Netherlands
The Murder Act passes into law in Great Britain - allows corpses of executed murderers to be given to the Company of Surgeons for dissection
US Congress passes Naturalization Act, requires 2-year residency
Pro-royalist uprising in Vendée region of France
Congress orders removal of Indians east of Mississippi to Louisiana
Territory of Orleans organizes in Louisiana Purchase
Franz Grillparzer's "Das Goldene Vliess" premieres in Vienna
The Book of Mormon is published in Palmyra, New York
1st Henley Royal Regatta on the river Thames, England
Joseph Francis from NYC, patents a corrugated sheet-iron lifeboat
Patent awarded for adhesive medicated plaster, precursor of band-aid
Decree regarding streets of Paris passed
New South Wales's first 1st-class game, v Victoria at Melbourne NSW wins
1st supposed sighting of Vulcan, a planet thought to orbit inside Mercury; it doesn't exist
Battle of La Glorieta Pass, New Mexico Territory (Apache Canyon, Pigeon's Ranch)
Voters in West Virginia approve gradual emancipation of slaves
Municipal elections bring revolutionaries to power in Paris who form the short lived Paris Commune government
7.8 earthquake shakes Owens Valley, California
Thomas J. Martin, of Alabama, receives a U.S. patent for a pipe and valve fire extinguisher system
Hastings College of Law founded in San Francisco, California
Thessaly is freed and becomes part of Greece again
1st modern, legal cremation held in England at Woking, Surrey
Eastman Film Co manufactures 1st commercial motion picture film
English cricketer Johnny Briggs takes 15-26 (7-17 & 8-11) at Newlands as South Africa are all out for 47 with Bernard Tancred not out on 26, then follow-on and are again all out for 43
King Alfonso plants pine sapling in Madrid, starts Spain's Arbor Day
Sabi Game Reserve opens in South Africa, the world's first officially designated game reserve; now part of Kruger National Park
1st edition The (Free) People (Netherlands, probably Amsterdam)
American Hotel opens in Amsterdam
August Strindberg's "Bjalb-jarle-ti" premieres in Stockholm
Russian military forces invade northern Persia to support Mohammad Ali Shah's coup d'état against the constitutional government in Persia and relieve the siege of Tabriz
US forbids immigration to criminals, anarchists, paupers and the sick
William H. Lewis appointed US Assistant Attorney General
Bulgaria captures Adrianople, ending the 1st Balkan War
Dayton, Ohio almost destroyed when Scioto, Miami and Muskingum River reach flood stage simultaneously

Stanley Cup Final, Denman Arena, Vancouver, BC: Barney Stanley scores 5 goals as Vancouver Millionaires beat Ottawa Senators, 12-3 for a 3-0 sweep of first non-challenge series; Vancouver first PCHA champions
British win a battle against Turks at Gaza
Stanley Cup Final, Seattle Ice Arena, Seattle, WA: Seattle Metropolitans (PCHA) beat Montreal Canadiens (NHL), 9-1 for a 3-1 series victory; first US team to win SC
Premiere of Bernard Shaw's play "Saint Joan" opens in London
ACD de Graeff appointed governor general of Dutch East Indies
First lip-reading tournament held in America
Alfred Hugenberg purchases German film company UFA
Gaumont-British Film Corporation forms
Congress appropriates $50,000 for Inter-American highway
Iraq & Trans-Jordan sign peace treaty
Leo Bentley bowls 3 consecutive perfect games in Lorain, Ohio
New Delhi replaces Calcutta as capital of British-Indies
Driving tests introduced in Britain
RJ Mitchell & Major Sorley discuss armament of Supermarine Spitfire
1st parliamentary debate on NZ radio
200" telescope lens shipped, Corning Glass Works, NY-Cal Tech
Mary Joyce ends a 1,000 mile trip by dog in Alaska
Spinach growers of Crystal City, Texas, erect statue of Popeye
William H. Hastie becomes 1st black federal judge (Virgin Islands)
Explosion of 20 tons of gelignite in a stone quarry at Easton, Pennsylvania, kills 21
Battle of Komandorski Islands, naval battle between American navy and Japanese Imperial forces in the Pacific Ocean, ends inconclusively
Elsie S. Ott is 1st woman to be awarded US Air Force Medal
705 British bombers attack Essen, Germany
Kamikaze attack on US battle fleet near Kerama Retto
US 7th Army crosses Rhine at Worms
Venray soccer team forms
Japanese film "Ugetsu" is released, directed by Kenji Mizoguchi, starring Masayuki Mori, Machiko Kyō and Kinuyo Tanaka
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Bikini Island
"The Ballad of Davy Crockett" version recorded by Bill Hayes becomes the #1 record in US
Medic Alert Foundation forms
Red Buttons stars as an angel on TV anthology series "Studio One" episode "Tale of St. Emergency"
The African Regroupment Party (PRA) is launched at a meeting in Paris
US Army launches America's third successful satellite, "Explorer III"
Test debut for Pakistani cricketer Mushtaq Mohammad v West Indies, aged 15 years
Iraq executes 30 after attack on Prime Minister Abd al-Karim Qasim
Orioles-Reds series planned for Havana is moved to Miami
USC captures NCAA swimming title
US Supreme Court backs 1-man-1-vote apportionment of seats in state legistature
Large-scale anti-Vietnam War protests take place in the United States, including in New York, Washington, D.C. and Chicago
21st Tony Awards: "The Homecoming" (play) & "Cabaret" (musical) win
"Marcus Welby, M.D.", starring Robert Young and James Brolin debuts as a TV movie on ABC-TV, prior to becoming a weekly series
Nuclear reactor Dodewaard in the Netherlands is commissioned
Soviet weather satellite Meteor 1 launched
500th nuclear explosion announced by the US since 1945
Folk singer Peter Yarrow (Peter, Paul & Mary) pleads guilty to "taking immoral liberties" with a 14-year-old girl and serves 3 months in prison; granted a presidential pardon in 1981
Golden Gate Park Conservatory made San Francisco city landmark
The Police (Northern Ireland) Act becomes law; the act provides for the disarmament of the Royal Ulster Constabulary (RUC) and the establishment of an RUC reserve force
"Cannon" starring William Conrad as a private detective premieres on CBS-TV; airs for 5 seasons
"The Benny Hill Show" tops TV ratings in UK
LA Lakers break the NBA wins record by winning 69 of 82 games (69-13), record stands for 24 years
William Whitelaw appointed as the first Secretary of State for Northern Ireland
Susan Shaw is first woman in 171 years in London's Stock exchange
TV soap opera "The Young & the Restless" premieres on CBS
Romanian communist party names party leader Ceausescu president
The Biological Weapons Convention enters into force.
Washington Capitals play record NHL 37th road game without a win & NHL record of 17 straight loses
AL approves purchase of Toronto franchise by LaBatt Brewing for $7M
Wings release "Wings at the Speed of Sound" album
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Delta State beat Louisiana State University 68-55 in Minneapolis
Focus on the Family is founded by Dr. James Dobson
Michigan State Spartans snaps Indiana State's 33-game winning streak
MLB San Diego Padres & San Francisco Giants announce plans to play exhibition series in Tokyo but Giant players reject it
OPEC makes full 14.5% oil price increase for 1979 effective on April 1
Bombay gets its 1st rock concert in 10 years (The Police)
Police & Albanian demonstrators battle in Kosovo, Yugoslavia
Soviet space mission Soyuz T-4 lands
Groundbreaking in Washington, D.C. for Vietnam Veterans Memorial
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Hyderabad beat Delhi on 1st innings to win Ranji Trophy
NASA launches Fltsatcom-6, it fails to reach orbit
National Federation of State High School Associations adopt the college 3 point shot (21 feet)
American Janet Evans swims 1,500m freestyle female world record 15:52.10 in the USA Spring Nationals in Orlando, Florida
Fuel pipe explodes under 58th street & Lexington Ave, NYC
Marc Camoletti's "Don't Dress for Dinner" premieres in London
Orlando Thunder beats San Antonio Riders in their 1st WLAF game 35-34
NHL NY Rangers clinch 1st NHL regular season championship in 50 years
German Gunda Niemann skates un-official world record 10 km ladies (14:22.60) in Calgary, Alberta
German Gunda Niemann skates world record 5 km ladies (7:03.26) in Calgary, Alberta
Mashonaland beat Mashonaland U-24 by 165 runs to win Logan Cup
Nabisco Dinah Shore Women's Golf, Mission Hills CC: Nanci Bowen wins her only major title, 1 stroke ahead of runner-up Susie Redman; her sole LPGA Tour victory
Schengen Agreement goes into effect
The International Monetary Fund approves a $10.2 billion loan for Russia
NHL announces Anaheim Mighty Ducks and Vancouver Canucks to open 1998 season in Japan
Thirty-nine bodies found in the Heaven's Gate cult suicides in Rancho Santa Fe, California
Oued Bouaicha massacre in Algeria; 52 people killed with axes and knives, 32 of them babies under the age of 2.
The "Melissa worm" infects Microsoft Word processing and email systems around the world

Kazakhstan's Prime Minister opens an oil pipeline from the giant Tengiz Field to the Russian port of Novorossiysk on Monday, giving the Central Asian producer its first direct link to international markets
Taiwanese government calls for people to demonstrate in Taipei against the Anti-Secession Law of the People's Republic of China. Around 200,000 to 300,000 attend the walk.
Burma's military junta officially names Naypyidaw, a new city in Mandalay Division, the new capital (Yangon previous capital)
In Scotland the prohibition of smoking in all substantially enclosed public places comes into force
Macky Sall elected as President of Senegal
Taavi Rõivas becomes Prime Minister of Estonia
Carrie Lam is first woman elected to lead Hong Kong by Beijing-influenced electoral commission
Mass protests in Chile over country's privatised pension system
US Federal Trade Commission confirms it will investigate Facebook over privacy concerns
US soldier receives world's first penis and scrotum transplant at Johns Hopkins University in Baltimore, Maryland
US, European Union and Ukraine expel more than 100 Russian diplomats in response to Russian use of nerve gas in UK
Charges of staging fake racist and homophobic attack dropped against Justin Smollett by Chicago prosecutors
European Parliament votes for changes to copyright laws, making internet companies responsible for copyright infringements
Maker of Oxyconti, Purdue Pharma agrees to pay Oklahoma $270 million settlement ahead of trial accusing the company of fueling the opioid epidemic
State of emergency declared in Rockland County, New York, due to measles epidemic with unvaccinated children banned from public spaces for 30 days after 153 cases
American cases of COVID-19 exceed all other countries on this date, with 81,578 cases and 1,180 deaths (New York Times)
Christchurch mosque gunman Brenton Tarrant pleads guilty to all 51 murders in New Zealand
Record number of Americans file for unemployment - 3.3 million according to US Department of Labor

WPL Cricket Final, Brabourne Stadium, Mumbai: Mumbai Indians win inaugural tournament beating Delhi Capitals by 7 wickets with 3 balls to spare; Player of the match: Nat Sciver-Brunt (Mumbai Indians) 60no

Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore is struck by a Singapore-flagged cargo ship that has lost power, causing the 1.6-mile-long, 47-year-old structure to completely collapse and killing six maintenance workers
Masaki Kashiwara is the first Japanese mathematician to be awarded Mathematics' greatest award, the Abel Prize, for his work on the theory of symmetry
Pope John XIX crownsConrad IIas Holy Roman Emperor, founding the Salian dynasty
William Caxtonprints his translation of Aesop's Fables
KingFrancis Ireturns from Spanish captivity to France
Charles IV of Spain abdicates in favor of his son,Ferdinand VII
Future Mormon church leaderJoseph Smithhas his "First Vision" in a wooded area of New York, according to Mormon scholars
Battle of Duck Lake:Louis Riel's forces defeat Canadian forces at Duck Lake, Saskatchewan, 1st engagement of the North-West Rebellion
Joe DiMaggiotakesTy Cobb's advice and replaces his 40 oz bat with a 36 oz one
NBC radio performance ofHoward Hanson's 3rd Symphony
German offensive in North Africa under GeneralErwin Rommel
Allied generalsDwight D. Eisenhower,Omar BradleyandGeorge S. Pattonlaunch attack at Remagen on the Rhine
British Prime MinisterWinston Churchilllooks over at the Rhine (near Ginsberg)
United States Air Force flag officially adopted by PresidentHarry S. Truman
Friedrich Durrenmatt's "Die Ehe des Herrn Mississippi" premieres in Munich
PopePaul VIpublishes encyclical Populorum progressio
Bangladesh (East Pakistan) underSheikh Mujibur Rahmandeclares independence from Pakistan
George ForemanTKOsKen Nortonin 2nd round  for the WBA, WBC and The Ring heavyweight boxing titles in Caracas, Venezuela
Ken Russell's film "Tommy", based on the rock opera by The Who, premieres in London;Roger Daltreyand Ann-Margret star,Tina TurnerandElton Johnare featured
Queen Elizabeth IIsent out the first royal email, from the Royal Signals and Radar Establishment
Elvis Costelloreleases his 1st record "Less Than Zero"
Israeli Prime MinisterMenachem Beginand Egyptian PresidentAnwar Sadatsign the Egypt-Israel Peace Treaty in Washington, D.C.
Paul McCartneyandStevie Wonderrelease the single "Ebony & Ivory" in the UK
PopeJohn Paul IIproclaims first ever World Youth Day
Geffen records signsGuns & Roses
62nd Academy Awards: "Driving Miss Daisy",Daniel Day-Lewis,Jessica Tandywin
Japanese filmmakerAkira Kurosawawins the Academy Award for Lifetime Achievement
Mike Tysonsentenced to 10 years in rape of Desiree Washington
AmericanBonnie Blairskates world record 500 m ladies (38.99 sec) in Calgary, Alberta
A jury in Michigan finds Dr.Jack Kevorkianguilty of second-degree murder for administering a lethal injection to a terminally ill man
Tom Snyder's final appearance as host of "The Late Late Show" on CBS, after 777 episodes
72nd Academy Awards: "American Beauty",Kevin Spacey&Hilary Swankwin
"Doctor Who" returns to BBC TV after 16 years with the debut of Christopher Eccleston as the Ninth Doctor andBillie Piperas Rose
Canadian filmmakerJames Cameronbecomes the first person to visit Challenger Deep, the deepest point on Earth in over 50 years
Ariana Grande's first single, "The Way", is released
Laureus World Sports Awards, Istana Budaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia: Sportsman:Sebastian Vettel; Sportswoman: Genzebe Dibaba; Team: Germany Men's National Football team
Richard III of England(1452-85) is reburied at Leicester Cathedral in England, after being discovered under a carpark in Leicester in 2012
US primary elections: Democratic candidateBernie Sanderswins Washington, Hawaii and Alaska
Anti-corruption protests in Russia result in hundreds arrested including opposition leaderAlexei Navalny
"The Black Panther" becomes the highest grossing superhero film in America earning $630.9m
E-commence groupAlibabaGroup and US car maker Ford unveil a car vending machine without sales people 5 stories high in Guangzhou, China
Porn star Stormy Daniels claims she had an affair withDonald Trumpin an interview with CBS's 60 Minutes and was later threatened to keep quiet
Michelle Obama's biography "Becoming" sells over 10 million copies according to its publisher Bertelsmann
StolenPablo Picassopainting "Buste de Femme" (Dora Maar) (1938) recovered after 20 years by Dutch art detective
US charges Venezuelan presidentNicolás Maduroand other officials with "narco-terrorism", by flooding the US with drugs
British rock bandGenesisperforms final concert of farewell tour at O2 Arena in London, England
US PresidentJoe Bidensays of Russian PresidentVladimir Putin, “For God’s sake, this man cannot remain in power" in unscripted remarks in Poland
Earthquake measuring 7.7 on the Richter scale destroys 90% of Caracas, Venezuela, and kills an estimated 15,000 to 20,000 people
First "Eichmann transport" to Auschwitz and Birkenau concentration camps
Allies led by US Marine Corps secure island of Iwo Jima from Imperial Japanese Army, after 18,000 Japanese & 6,000 Americans killed
Dr. Jonas Salkannounces that he has successfully tested a vaccine to prevent polio, clinical trials begin the following year
30th Academy Awards-"The Bridge on the River Kwai" wins Best Picture, Joanne Woodward &Alec Guinnesswin Best Actress and Best Actor, respectively
